title: Melted Sweet Argentinian Goats Cheese Empanadas
description: A sweet and shattering Argentinian dish featuring goats cheese, perfect for a quick fix, with anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of goats cheese in the center of each dough circle.
Drizzle a little honey over the cheese.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each empanada.
Drizzle the tops with olive oil and sprinkle with salt, black pepper, and turmeric.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the empanadas from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
